name: boxes
title: boxes
summary: textmode box- and comment drawing filter
description:
textmode box- and comment drawing filter
Boxes is extremely configurable filter for adding and removing ASCII art
(comments, for example) around chunks of text.  Most modern text editors
support filtering text through external filters.  Boxes is such a filter.

command_examples: Boxes also combines nicely with other tools. Try
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use boxes because textmode box- and comment drawing filter.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use boxes as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `boxes [options] [infile [outfile]]` as documented upstream for boxes.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `echo "Good Bye World!" | boxes -d nuke` as documented upstream for boxes.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `Boxes also combines nicely with other tools. Try` as documented upstream for boxes.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `figlet "boxes . . . !" | lolcat -f | boxes -d unicornthink` as documented upstream for boxes.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair boxes with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `boxes --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
